fields.description:
## Why
Bug c8ed (outbound depends_on link direction) shipped because the link-sync tests exercised
the relation vocabulary with a single representative (`blocks`, ~19×; `depends_on` 0×), used
mechanical stub assertions, and the only semantic round-trip oracle
(`tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`) is gated behind live Jira. An audit found
three OTHER gaps of the same shape, all in the link-sync seam.

## What
Close the sibling gaps by PARAMETRIZING the link tests over the FULL relation vocabulary, so
every current AND future relation is covered (no more single-representative blind spots).
Test-only change; no production behavior changes.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py`: parametrize outbound `_diff_links`
  emission over ALL relations — assert `(jira_type, swap)` for each MAPPED relation
  (blocks→Blocks/False, depends_on→Blocks/True, relates_to→Relates/False) and assert `[]` for
  each UNMAPPED/skip relation (duplicates, supersedes, discovered_from). [Findings 1 + 3]
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_removal_sync.py`: add a REMOVE case where a
  Jira issue carries an OUTWARD `Blocks` link whose local `depends_on` dep was removed; assert
  the emitted mutation is `{action: remove, relation: depends_on}` (the untested branch of the
  REMOVE path's own inward/outward direction logic at outbound_links.py:193-202). [Finding 2]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An outbound emission test is parametrized over all 6 relations: the 3 mapped ones assert
  the exact `(type, swap)` tuple; the 3 unmapped ones assert an empty mutation list.
  Proof: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py -q`.
- [ ] A REMOVE test covers outward-`Blocks`→`depends_on`. Proof:
  `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_removal_sync.py -q`.
- [ ] The parametrized `depends_on` case asserts the discriminating orientation (`swap is True`
  on the differ side, and the SWAPPED `(to_key, issue_key)` set_relationship call on the applier
  side) — not merely that a mutation exists — so a re-drop of the c8ed swap fix would fail it
  (a real guard, not a tautology). Proof:
  `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`.
- [ ] `make lint`/`make typecheck` pass; no production source files change.
